title: How I Will do the Ultimate Kayak

transcript.text: Hello, hello. Well, I do the UKT. I will paddle. Keep paddling and keep paddling until I get there. So the important thing is to make sure that we can be out there in the middle of the ocean, 100 miles from anywhere, 50 miles from anywhere, and feel safe and be safe and be happy and keep paddling.

So let me just show you my boat real quick. I think that's the best way to explain it. So here's the boat, right? Now, people may be, or I guess people have said, because I've been on some kayaking forums, and they've said, you know, some people think that I'm going to die, and I get rid of the legend, but I actually have someone who's trying to come take my ass.
But, you know, only one of those people has ever actually given me a reason, an example, and they were at the beginning of kayaking in Alaska without knowing how to flip or something like a roll.
I have yet to tell people who've actually done trips about my trip, and have them discourage me. To be honest, I'd be kind of surprised if they did.

The big problem that some people at least call themselves sea kayakers, sorry if that sounds a little bit harsh, but is that I guess I don't have any experience if they've been on the ocean, and I guess they have for 20 years or so.

But the thing is, this is not just the same thing as backyard sea kayaking. Somebody said, you know, you don't know what you're talking about, kayak 40 miles, and then you'll know what I'm talking about.
And I was like, I've never kayaked less than 40 miles, really. You know, I just, anyways, actually, no, I mean, I've been on one dugout canoe trip, and I did a ton of miles in two days.

I was the second day all by myself in 16 miles in a storm, crashing inside of my boat, so, you know, and then I kiked 50 miles like two months ago, and it's like, and then this other time I kiked 25 miles, and then another time, like, that far, 20 miles.
But anyway, 40 miles is no big deal to me. So anyway, how are we going to do it? Well, the dangers.
Let's see. First danger, getting tired. I'm going to show you what I'm going to do about that. In there is my food. We can store, like, two weeks' worth of food in there.
Water. There's, like, right now I think I have seven or eight of these guys, and I could get more.
Filled up, that's a lot of water. That's 90 ounces of water each.
And then another thing that people like to bring up, though, why I'm going to die is I guess I'm going to, like, tip over.
So, what am I going to do about tipping over? I've got outriggers. Who else uses outriggers? Just us.
Now, if we tip over with those outriggers, it doesn't matter how good of a roller I am.
We're not going to roll back. We're going to have to get out of the boat.
So how are we going to do that? See those handles? Yes. Those will keep us attached to the boat.
But we've both got some. There's some more.
And then we'll figure out how to flip that thing over. It'll be easy to do, I'm sure.
I'm not going to be able to do any field testing with my boat and the water before I leave because that little car right there ain't working.
But it's cool. I don't want to go out anyway because every time I go out I'll probably lose stuff.
I know what you're thinking. How do you know you're not going to lose stuff on the actual trip?
Because I'll be with Tony and he'll make sure.
So, let's see. What's another danger people say? Sharks. We're going to even buy a shark.
Well, let's see. What are we going to do about that?
Check it out.
My entire boat is painted with black stripes.
Black stripes scare sharks away. So there you go.
Plus I've got a 16-foot boat.
What shark's going to go after a big 16-foot boat with outriggers attached?
You'd have to be crazy.
Now, what if we actually do get bitten by a shark?
Well, this boat is made out of the same stuff that river kayaks are made out of.
Made out of bomb-proof plastic.
I could drag this thing behind my car to Miami and it would still make the trip.
So, let's see. What else? What's another danger?
People like to talk about, I guess, pirates and thieves stealing all my stuff and then I run out of money and I can't buy any more stuff.
That's actually the thing I'm scared about them of.
So, what am I going to do about that?
Well, every one of my boxes has its own padlock.
You see?
Now, every time I dock, I can take these boxes off.
And, you can see the little hook there.
I can run a cable through all of those and then attach it to my boat.
And, then, put it underneath my skirt, you know?
And, I'm going to be keeping my boat and paying my boat to stay in docks and stuff, right?
So, I don't think anybody's going to sneak into the dock with a lock bolt cutter.
I can also lock my hatches shut.
You can put more valuable stuff in there and I can lock that shut like that.
And, then, wrap the cable completely around the boat.
That's for that hatch and the hatch up front.
Now, what's another danger?
Sometimes people sleep and they underestimate the tides and the tides take your boat away.
Well, I've got the hammock situation.
See that pole up front?
Attach the hammock to the pole up front.
That pole back there.
And, I'm sleeping on my boat.

Getting hit by boats.
